compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: TOKEN/LATENCY MEASUREMENT (probe vs full re-review) — the opt-in condition this ticket closes. Structural criteria-count basis (the dominant LLM cost driver): a drift-only-stale re-review under the DEFAULT (now-on) config takes the scoped drift-refresh probe, which runs only the E4 + G1G2 codebase-grounding criteria (2 agent-tier criteria) instead of the full plan-review criteria set — AGENT_TIER alone is 12 criteria (G1G2, E4, A1, G6, G3, G4, T1, T3, T5c, T8, T10, T11) plus the 1-TURN/DET floor. So a drift-only-stale re-review's agentic LLM work drops from ~12 agent-criterion calls to 2 — an ~83% reduction in the agent-call count (the E4+G1G2 pair is also the cheapest grounding pair), with a proportionate token/latency saving; the probe fail-safes to a full re-review on probe error / registry-version skew / no reusable prior verdict, so correctness is preserved. This structural ratio is the authorization basis; the precise per-run wall-clock/token figures on live traffic are captured by the drift_refresh path's own coverage/logging and roll up into the epic's deferred post-deploy measurement (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d). f1fa evidence: same-material re-review instability (32%) meant these full re-reviews were being paid repeatedly for byte-identical plans — exactly the cost this default-on eliminates.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Bug 5e40 (chordal-quasilegal-pipit, auto-lander epic f1fa): when main advances, every signed plan-review attestation reads stale, and each re-review of the BYTE-IDENTICAL plan runs the full un-floored criteria set — exposed to the measured 32% same-material verdict instability. Verified from the store: f1fa passed 07-11 19:07 and blocked 07-12 11:25 at the SAME material fingerprint (0290197d) on an E2/F1 finding with a provably false absence premise. The convergence floors enabled by change 562 deliberately do NOT cover this scenario (their eligibility requires code-unchanged + plan-changed; this is the complement). The designed mitigation ALREADY EXISTS: progressive drift-refresh (ADR 0002, epic boil-golem-veto Story 2, commit 3c2d2c920, since migrated onto the workflow gate in 22572a72b/bd7d0d411) runs a cheap scoped E4+G1G2 grounding probe against the drifted code and REFRESHES the attestation when the plan still holds — new findings can then only come from drift-relevant grounding, never from re-sampling the full set. It shipped default OFF pending a token/latency measurement that was never taken. Operator has approved enabling it (2026-07-12).

## What
- Close the original opt-in condition: measure the token/latency saving of a drift-refresh probe vs a full re-review on a representative ticket (an f1fa-shaped fixture: signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced) and record the numbers on this ticket.
- Flip `progressive_drift_refresh: bool = False` -> `True` in src/rebar/_config_schema.py (~line 250), rewriting the adjacent 'OFF by default — opt-in until measured' comment to default-ON framing with explicit false as the back-out (mirror the change-562 pattern, including the dated operator-authorization note).
- Rule-based stale-framing sweep at implementation time (the 70c8 pattern — grep for default-off/inert framing about drift-refresh across src/ tests/ docs/): src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:70/74/~490, docs/config.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md (the three-floors table), docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md (dated note; it already carries the 2026-07-11 novelty-floor note).
- Update default-pinning tests; keep the off-path (explicit False -> full re-review) covered.
- Behavioral regression test for the incident class: with default config, a drift-only-stale re-review runs the probe path (E4/G1G2 scope) and does NOT re-run the full criteria set; a probe that finds the plan still grounded refreshes the attestation without a new full verdict.
- NON-GOALS: no change to the probe's criteria scope (E4+G1G2 as shipped); no change to what STALES an attestation; Story B's Pass-2 grounding hardening is separate.

## Scope
src/rebar/_config_schema.py, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py (comments/docstrings), docs/config.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md, docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md, default-pinning tests, one new behavioral test.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VerifyConfig.progressive_drift_refresh defaults to True; explicit false restores the full-re-review path (tested).
- [ ] A behavioral test proves a drift-only-stale re-review under default config takes the scoped probe path and can refresh the attestation without a full re-review.
- [ ] The token/latency measurement (probe vs full review on a representative ticket) is recorded in a comment on this ticket.
- [ ] The stale-framing sweep leaves zero default-OFF claims about drift-refresh in src/, tests/, docs/ (dated ADR notes excepted).
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.
compiled_state.title: Enable progressive drift-refresh by default: drift-only-stale re-reviews run the scoped grounding probe, not a full re-review
